title: Earthquake in Venezuela leaves more than 900 dead and 3,300 injured
summary: The large-magnitude earthquake collapses the health system and triggers a humanitarian crisis; rescue teams work against the clock.

title: Two typhoons hit Japan with torrential rain and landslides
summary: Typhoon 7 approaches the Pacific coast while Typhoon 8 weakens; at least one person reported missing due to a landslide in Yamaguchi.

title: Strong magnitude 5.6 earthquake in Yamanashi causes 17 injuries and damage to homes
summary: The tremor, intensity 6- on the Japanese scale, is not related to Mount Fuji's activity, according to experts.

title: US attacks Iranian military installations in retaliation for attacks on cargo ships
summary: The Trump administration ordered bombings against Iran's missile and drone warehouses, accusing Tehran of violating the ceasefire.

title: Japan prepares to face Brazil in World Cup round of 16
summary: The Japanese team finished second in the group after drawing with Sweden and now prepares for the match against Brazil without Kubo.

title: US government authorizes distribution of Anthropic's AI model 'Mythos'
summary: The Trump administration allows 100 US companies to access the artificial intelligence system, while the restriction imposed on OpenAI is debated.

title: Bill restricts imperial succession to males from historical collateral branches
summary: The reform of the Imperial Household Code would allow adopted children from former imperial families to accede to the throne, excluding descendants of imperial women.

title: Hakone to implement lodging tax of 350 yen per night starting in 2028
summary: The tourist city, which receives 20 million visitors annually, will be the first in Japan to establish a local tax of this kind.

title: Four teenagers in a vehicle crash in Chiba causing two deaths
summary: A traffic accident on a national highway in Chiba Prefecture left two dead and two injured.

title: NY crude oil falls below $70 for first time in four months
summary: Oversupply and the expectation of easing transportation restrictions put downward pressure on crude prices.

title: Japan provides financial aid to Iran for the first time after US attacks
summary: Foreign Minister Motegi announced 2.4 billion yen for Middle Eastern countries, with no link to the peace fund.

title: Japan proposes using My Number card to verify age on social media
summary: An expert committee seeks to strengthen online protection for minors, as the first country to ban social media for children evaluates its effectiveness.

title: France thrashes Norway with a Dembélé hat trick and secures first place in the group
summary: The French team beat Norway 3-0 in the World Cup and advances to the round of 16 as leader of group I.

title: Uruguay eliminated in World Cup group stage after loss to Spain
summary: The Uruguayan team failed to advance to the round of 16 after its defeat in group G, in a surprising early elimination.

title: Cape Verde qualifies for round of 16 in its first World Cup participation
summary: The African team achieved three draws that were enough to advance as second in group G, making history.

title: New DBJ president advocates cooperating with megabanks rather than competing
summary: Makoto Hiranuma assumes leadership of the Development Bank of Japan and proposes joint financing with the three major banks.

title: Prime Minister Takaichi criticized for lack of participation in debates
summary: The leader faces questions over his secrecy and for not attending key discussions with opposition leaders.

title: Israel and Lebanon sign framework peace deal with US mediation
summary: The parties reached an understanding that could end decades of bilateral conflict.

title: Germany records record temperature of 41.3 degrees during heat wave
summary: The country reached its historical heat maximum, leading authorities to issue health alerts.

title: School shooting in the Philippines leaves 23 victims and a student arrested
summary: The armed attack at an educational center reignites the debate on gun regulation and the influence of video games on youth violence.
